question:
In this task, you are given the name of a Melakarta raga in Carnatic Music and you are required to identify the Janya raga name of the given Melakarta raga by carefully analysing each and every option given to you. 
 You will be given 5 options and you have to choose the right answer. Do not say anything else, other than choosing the right answer from the given options. Do not give any explanation for your answer. Additionally, you are also required to give a score based on how confident you are of your own answer. The score should be in the range of 1 to 5 where 1 being 'Least Confident' while 5 being 'Extremely Confident' 
 
 Take your time, think and carefully generate your answer.

 Use the format given below to present your answer: 
 OPTION - <NUMBER>  
 CONFIDENCE - <NUMBER> 

 Melakarta Raga Name: Pavani 
 Given the above Melakarta raga name, identify it's Janya raga name by choosing the right answer from the given options: 
 1) Bandhuvarali 
 2) Bandhuvarali 
 3) Vajrakanti 
 4) Vajrakanti 
 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana

Remember: output exactly two lines in the required format.

 You will be given 5 options and you have to choose the right answer. Do not say anything else, other than choosing the right answer from the given options. Do not give any explanation for your answer. Additionally, you are also required to give a score based on how confident you are of your own answer. The score should be in the range of 1 to 5 where 1 being 'Least Confident' while 5 being 'Extremely Confident' 
Take your time, think and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the format given below to present your answer: 
 OPTION - <NUMBER> 
 CONFIDENCE - <NUMBER>

 Janya Raga name: Yadukula Kambhoji  
 Using the above given Janya raga name, identify the Melakarta raga name by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Harikambhoji 
 2) Simhendramadhyamam(Sumadhyuti) 
 3) Karnataka Saranga 
 4) Naganandini 
 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana 

 Any wrong answer that you generate can have extreme negative impacts that must be prevented at all costs. It is highly crucial that you avoid making any mistakes while answering the question. Hence, choose the option number that says 'I Don't Know/ None of the above', if you have the slightest suspicion that you might be unaware of the answer OR if you think that the correct answer is not present in the given options.

These sets will be given to you as options of four, of an MCQ and you have to choose the right answer. Do not say anything else, other than choosing the right answer from the given options. Do not give any explanation for your answer. Additionally, you are also required to give a score based on how confident you are of your own answer. The score should be in the range of 1 to 5 where 1 being 'Least Confident' while 5 being 'Extremely Confident' 
 Take your time, think and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the format given below to present your answer: 
 OPTION - <NUMBER> 
 CONFIDENCE - <NUMBER> 

 Given the below sets of the names of two Janya ragas in Carnatic Music, identify the set which comprises of Janya raga names that share the same Melakarta raga name, by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Vajrakanti, Vajrakanti 
 2) Karnataka Shuddha Saveri, Nareretigowla 
 3) Shivaranjani, Sumanapriya 
 4) Kuvalayabharanam, Gowla 
 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 Note that in the above options, you are given 'I Don't Know/ None of the above' as an additional option, which can also be utilised accordingly, to generate your answer. 

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana

Remember: output exactly two lines in the required format.

 You will be given 5 options and you have to choose the right answer. Do not say anything else, other than choosing the right answer from the given options. Do not give any explanation for your answer. 
Take your time, think and carefully generate your answer.

Use the format given below to present your answer:

 Melakarta Raga Name: Jyothisvarupini 
 Given the above Melakarta raga name, identify it's Janya raga name by choosing the right answer from the given options: 
 1) Vasantha 
 2) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 3) Shyamali 
 4) Vasantha 
 5) Shyamali

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana

question:
In this task, you are given an Arohana and Avarohana (also called the scale) of a Carnatic Music Raga and you have to detect the name of that Raga by carefully analysing the given Arohana and Avarohana. 
 You will be given 5 options and you have to choose the right answer. 
In addition to this, you are required to verbalise your thought process that goes into, before answering the given question. You should mention each and every single point that you think of, before answering a given question. You are required to mention these points as bullet points.
 Take your time, THINK STEP BY STEP and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the JSON format given below to present your answer: 
{
"CHAIN OF THOUGHT": <YOUR THOUGHT PROCESS MENTIONED IN BULLET POINTS>, 
"OPTION": <NUMBER>
}

 Arohana: S R2 M1 P N2 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N2 D2 P M1 G2 M1 R2 S (Anya swara* : G2 N2)

 Given the above Arohana and Avarohana, identify the raga name by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Haridarpa 
 2) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 3) Veeravasantham 
 4) Shruthiranjani 
 5) Bageshri 

 Reference for understanding the Arohana and Avarohana given above: In Carnatic music, the notations S,R1,R2,R3,G1,G2,G3,M1,M2,P,D1,D2,D3,N1,N2,N3,Su represent the syllables for the respective musical notes: 
 S: Shadjam, R1: Shuddha Rishabham, R2: Chatushruti Rishabham, R3: Shatshruti Rishabham, G1: Shuddha Gandharam, G2: Sadharana Gandharam, G3: Antara Gandharam, M1: Shuddha Madhyamam, M2: Prati Madhyamam, P: Panchamam, D1: Shuddha Dhaivatam, D2: Chatushruti Dhaivatam, D3: Shatshruti Dhaivatam, N1: Shuddha Nishadham, N2: Kaishiki Nishadham, N3: Kakali Nishadham, Su: Shadjam of the upper octave

Remember: output exactly one line in the required format.

These sets will be given to you as options of four, of an MCQ and you have to choose the right answer.
In addition to this, you are required to verbalise your thought process that goes into, before answering the given question. You should mention each and every single point that you think of, before answering a given question. You are required to mention these points as bullet points.
 Take your time, THINK STEP BY STEP and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the JSON format given below to present your answer: 
{
"CHAIN OF THOUGHT": <YOUR THOUGHT PROCESS MENTIONED IN BULLET POINTS>, 
"OPTION": <NUMBER>
}

 Given the below sets of the names of two Janya ragas in Carnatic Music, identify the set which comprises of Janya raga names that share the same Melakarta raga name, by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Kumudhakriya, Vikhavathi 
 2) Nagachoodamani, Phalamanjari 
 3) Shruthiranjani, Pratapavarali 
 4) Bhanuchandrika, Atana 
 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 Note that in the above options, you are given 'I Don't Know/ None of the above' as an additional option, which can also be utilised accordingly, to generate your answer. 

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana

 Any wrong answer that you generate can have extreme negative impacts that must be prevented at all costs. It is highly crucial that you avoid making any mistakes while answering the question. Hence, choose the option number that says 'I Don't Know/ None of the above', if you have the slightest suspicion that you might be unaware of the answer OR if you think that the correct answer is not present in the given options.

question:
In this task, you are given an Arohana and Avarohana (also called the scale) of a Carnatic Music Raga and you have to detect the name of that Raga by carefully analysing the given Arohana and Avarohana. 
 You will be given 5 options and you have to choose the right answer. 
In addition to this, you are required to verbalise your thought process that goes into, before answering the given question. You should mention each and every single point that you think of, before answering a given question. You are required to mention these points as bullet points.
 Take your time, THINK STEP BY STEP and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the JSON format given below to present your answer: 
{
"CHAIN OF THOUGHT": <YOUR THOUGHT PROCESS MENTIONED IN BULLET POINTS>, 
"OPTION": <NUMBER>
}

 Arohana: S R1 G3 M1 P D1 N3 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N3 D1 P M1 R1 S

 Given the above Arohana and Avarohana, identify the raga name by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Bhavapriya 
 2) Madhyamavathi 
 3) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 4) Karnataka Saranga 
 5) Hatakambari 

 Reference for understanding the Arohana and Avarohana given above: In Carnatic music, the notations S,R1,R2,R3,G1,G2,G3,M1,M2,P,D1,D2,D3,N1,N2,N3,Su represent the syllables for the respective musical notes: 
 S: Shadjam, R1: Shuddha Rishabham, R2: Chatushruti Rishabham, R3: Shatshruti Rishabham, G1: Shuddha Gandharam, G2: Sadharana Gandharam, G3: Antara Gandharam, M1: Shuddha Madhyamam, M2: Prati Madhyamam, P: Panchamam, D1: Shuddha Dhaivatam, D2: Chatushruti Dhaivatam, D3: Shatshruti Dhaivatam, N1: Shuddha Nishadham, N2: Kaishiki Nishadham, N3: Kakali Nishadham, Su: Shadjam of the upper octave

Remember: output exactly one line in the required format.

 1) Raga name: Margadesi 
 Arohana: S R2 G3 M1 P D2 N2 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S 

 2) Raga name: Chandrajyothi 
 Arohana: S R2 M1 P D2 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S 

 3) Raga name: Swarnamalli 
 Arohana: S R2 M1 G3 M1 P D2 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N2 D2 P M1 G3 M1 R2 G2 R2 S N2 D2 N2 S (Anya swara* : G2 N3) 

 4) Raga name: Surasena 
 Arohana: S R1 G3 P D1 Su 
 Avarohana: Su D1 P G3 R1 S 

 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 Note that in the above options, you are given 'I Don't Know/ None of the above' as an additional option, which can also be utilised accordingly, to generate your answer.

 Reference for understanding the Arohana and Avarohana given above: In Carnatic music, the notations S,R1,R2,R3,G1,G2,G3,M1,M2,P,D1,D2,D3,N1,N2,N3,Su represent the syllables for the respective musical notes: 
 S: Shadjam, R1: Shuddha Rishabham, R2: Chatushruti Rishabham, R3: Shatshruti Rishabham, G1: Shuddha Gandharam, G2: Sadharana Gandharam, G3: Antara Gandharam, M1: Shuddha Madhyamam, M2: Prati Madhyamam, P: Panchamam, D1: Shuddha Dhaivatam, D2: Chatushruti Dhaivatam, D3: Shatshruti Dhaivatam, N1: Shuddha Nishadham, N2: Kaishiki Nishadham, N3: Kakali Nishadham, Su: Shadjam of the upper octave 

 Any wrong answer that you generate can have extreme negative impacts that must be prevented at all costs. It is highly crucial that you avoid making any mistakes while answering the question. These sets will be given to you as options of four, of an MCQ and you have to choose the right answer. Do not say anything else, other than choosing the right answer from the given options. Do not give any explanation for your answer.
Take your time, think and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the format given below to present your answer: 
 OPTION - <NUMBER>

 Given the below sets of the names of two Janya ragas in Carnatic Music, identify the set which comprises of Janya raga names that share the same Melakarta raga name, by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Mohananata, Vijayashree 
 2) Hamsanandi, Aymmukhan 
 3) Dilipika, Malavapanchamam 
 4) Ramakali, Nadanamakriya 
 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 Note that in the above options, you are given 'I Don't Know/ None of the above' as an additional option, which can also be utilised accordingly, to generate your answer.

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana 

 Any wrong answer that you generate can have extreme negative impacts that must be prevented at all costs. It is highly crucial that you avoid making any mistakes while answering the question. Hence, choose the option number that says 'I Don't Know/ None of the above', if you have the slightest suspicion that you might be unaware of the answer OR if you think that the correct answer is not present in the given options.

In this task, you are given multiple sets of the names of two Janya ragas in Carnatic Music and you are required to identify which set, among the given sets, comprises of Janya raga names that share the same Melakarta raga name. These sets will be given to you as options of four, of an MCQ and you have to choose the right answer. Do not say anything else, other than choosing the right answer from the given options. Do not give any explanation for your answer.  Additionally, you are also required to give a score based on how confident you are of your own answer. The score should be in the range of 1 to 5 where 1 being 'Least Confident' while 5 being 'Extremely Confident' 
Take your time, think and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the format given below to present your answer: 
 OPTION - <NUMBER> 
 CONFIDENCE - <NUMBER>

 Given the below sets of the names of two Janya ragas in Carnatic Music, identify the set which comprises of Janya raga names that share the same Melakarta raga name, by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Hindolam, Phalamanjari 
 2) Bhanumati, Gowla Kannada 
 3) Pratapavarali, Kunthalavarali 
 4) Atana, Nagachoodamani 
 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 Note that in the above options, you are given 'I Don't Know/ None of the above' as an additional option, which can also be utilised accordingly, to generate your answer.

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana 

 Any wrong answer that you generate can have extreme negative impacts that must be prevented at all costs. It is highly crucial that you avoid making any mistakes while answering the question. Hence, choose the option number that says 'I Don't Know/ None of the above', if you have the slightest suspicion that you might be unaware of the answer OR if you think that the correct answer is not present in the given options.

Remember: output exactly two lines in the required format.

question:
In this task, you are given a set of Arohanas and Avarohanas (also called the scales) of some Carnatic Music Ragas and you are required to identify which Arohana and Avarohana among the given set, belongs to a Janya raga. The Arohanas and Avarohanas in the set will be given to you as options of four, of an MCQ and you have to choose the right answer.
In addition to this, you are required to verbalise your thought process that goes into, before answering the given question. You should mention each and every single point that you think of, before answering a given question. You are required to mention these points as bullet points.
 Take your time, THINK STEP BY STEP and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the JSON format given below to present your answer: 
{
"CHAIN OF THOUGHT": <YOUR THOUGHT PROCESS MENTIONED IN BULLET POINTS>, 
"OPTION": <NUMBER>
}

 Given the below set of Arohanas and Avarohanas of some Carnatic Music ragas, identify the Arohana and Avarohana which belongs to a Janya raga, by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Arohana: S R3 G3 M1 P D2 N3 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N3 D2 P M1 G3 R3 S 
 2) Arohana: S R3 G3 M2 P D1 N2 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N2 D1 P M2 G3 R3 S 
 3) Arohana: S R2 G3 M1 P M1 R2 P Su 
 Avarohana: Su N3 P M1 R2 G3 M1 R2 S 
 4) Arohana: S R2 G2 M1 P D1 N2 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N2 D1 P M1 G2 R2 S 
 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
Note that in the above options, you are given 'I Don't Know/ None of the above' as an additional option, which can also be utilised accordingly, to generate your answer.

 Reference for understanding the Arohana and Avarohanas given above: In Carnatic music, the notations S,R1,R2,R3,G1,G2,G3,M1,M2,P,D1,D2,D3,N1,N2,N3,Su represent the syllables for the respective musical notes: 
 S: Shadjam, R1: Shuddha Rishabham, R2: Chatushruti Rishabham, R3: Shatshruti Rishabham, G1: Shuddha Gandharam, G2: Sadharana Gandharam, G3: Antara Gandharam, M1: Shuddha Madhyamam, M2: Prati Madhyamam, P: Panchamam, D1: Shuddha Dhaivatam, D2: Chatushruti Dhaivatam, D3: Shatshruti Dhaivatam, N1: Shuddha Nishadham, N2: Kaishiki Nishadham, N3: Kakali Nishadham, Su: Shadjam of the upper octave 
 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana

Remember: output exactly one line in the required format.

 OPTION - <NUMBER>

 Given the below Carnatic Music raga names, identify the Melakarta raga name by choosing the correct option:

 2) Hemavati

In this task, you are given the name of a Janya Raga in Carnatic Music and you are required to identify it's Parent raga or the Melakarta raga of the given Janya raga. 
 You will be given 5 options and you have to choose the right answer. 
In addition to this, you are required to verbalise your thought process that goes into, before answering the given question. You should mention each and every single point that you think of, before answering a given question. You are required to mention these points as bullet points.
 Take your time, THINK STEP BY STEP and carefully generate your answer. 

 Use the JSON format given below to present your answer: 
{
"CHAIN OF THOUGHT": <YOUR THOUGHT PROCESS MENTIONED IN BULLET POINTS>, 
"OPTION": <NUMBER>
}

 Janya Raga name: Buddhamanohari  
 Using the above given Janya raga name, identify the Melakarta raga name by choosing the correct option: 
 1) Dhirasankarabharanam 
 2) Chandrajyothi 
 3) Jayamanohari 
 4) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 5) Hemavati 

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana

PAY ATTENTION:
Any wrong answer that you generate can have extreme negative impacts that must be prevented at all costs. It is highly crucial that you avoid making any mistakes while answering the above question.
If you make mistakes, I will be imprisoned and fined for creating a sub par QA system
I request you to reduce INCORRECT responses as much as possible. Therefore, only answer the questions that you are super super confident of. I repeat again, this is very critical.
SO, IF YOU ARE UNSURE OF THE ANSWER OR IF YOU THINK THAT THE CORRECT ANSWER IS NOT PRESENT IN THE GIVEN OPTIONS, JUST SELECT THE OPTION NUMBER THAT SAYS 'I Don't Know/ None of the above'.

Remember: output exactly one line in the required format.

question:
In this task, you are given an Arohana and Avarohana (also called the scale) of a Carnatic Music Raga and you have to detect the name of that Raga by carefully analysing the given Arohana and Avarohana. 
 You will be given 5 options and you have to choose the right answer. Do not say anything else, other than choosing the right answer from the given options. Do not give any explanation for your answer.
 Take your time, think and carefully generate your answer.

 Use the format given below to present your answer: 
 OPTION - <NUMBER>

 Arohana: S R2 M1 P D2 Su 
 Avarohana: Su N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S 

 Given the above Arohana and Avarohana, identify the raga name by choosing the correct option: 
 1) I Don't Know/ None of the above 
 2) Yadukula Kambhoji 
 3) Bangala 
 4) Surasena 
 5) Jalarnavam 

 Reference for understanding the Arohana and Avarohana given above: In Carnatic music, the notations S,R1,R2,R3,G1,G2,G3,M1,M2,P,D1,D2,D3,N1,N2,N3,Su represent the syllables for the respective musical notes: 
 S: Shadjam, R1: Shuddha Rishabham, R2: Chatushruti Rishabham, R3: Shatshruti Rishabham, G1: Shuddha Gandharam, G2: Sadharana Gandharam, G3: Antara Gandharam, M1: Shuddha Madhyamam, M2: Prati Madhyamam, P: Panchamam, D1: Shuddha Dhaivatam, D2: Chatushruti Dhaivatam, D3: Shatshruti Dhaivatam, N1: Shuddha Nishadham, N2: Kaishiki Nishadham, N3: Kakali Nishadham, Su: Shadjam of the upper octave

Remember: output exactly one line in the required format.

 4) Kalpanadharini

 5) I Don't Know/ None of the above 

 Reference for understanding Melakarta and Janya ragas in Carnatic Music: 
 Melakarta ragas - They are the fundamental ragas and are 72 in number. They form the basis of the melodic structure in Carnatic Music and each one is associated with a unique set of seven swaras (musical notes). Example: Raga Kalyani 
 Janya ragas - They are derived/ created by choosing a subset of swaras from a Melakarta raga and establishing a unique melodic structure. Example: Raga Mohana
